questions.question: What is the term for a path in a graph that visits each edge exactly once?
options: A) Eulerian Circuit
B) Hamiltonian Path
C) Euler Graph
D) Hamiltonian Circuit
questions.explanation: A Eulerian Circuit is a path in a connected graph that visits each edge exactly once.

questions.question: In a circuit, what is the number of vertices that must be visited to complete the circuit?
options: D) None of the above
questions.explanation: In a circuit, the number of vertices that must be visited to complete the circuit is equal to the number of edges in the circuit.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a Hamiltonian path and a Hamiltonian circuit?
options: A) A Hamiltonian path starts and ends at the same vertex
B) A Hamiltonian path can visit each edge twice
C) A Hamiltonian path visits each vertex only once
D) A Hamiltonian path visits each edge only once
questions.explanation: A Hamiltonian path visits each vertex only once, whereas a Hamiltonian circuit visits each edge only once and starts and ends at the same vertex.

questions.question: What is an Euler graph?
options: A) A graph with a Hamiltonian circuit
B) A graph with a Hamiltonian path
C) A graph in which all vertices have even degree
D) A graph with a cycle that visits each edge exactly once
questions.explanation: An Euler graph is a graph in which all vertices have even degree, making it possible to draw a circuit that visits each edge exactly once.

questions.question: What is the condition for a graph to have a Hamiltonian circuit?
options: A) The graph must be connected
B) The graph must have a Hamiltonian path
C) The graph must have at least 3 vertices
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A graph must be connected and have at least 3 vertices to have a Hamiltonian circuit.
